'''Sparky''' was [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo's]] [[R5-series astromech droid]] during her early tenure with [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]] with the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].<ref name="Onslaught" /> Sparky was destroyed<ref name="Balance Point">''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]''</ref> a [[26 ABY/Legends|year into]]<ref name="TERC" /> the conflict when Jaina strayed too close to an exploding [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] ship in her [[T-65XJ X-wing starfighter]] at the [[Battle of Kalarba]]. Jaina was distraught at the loss of her droid,<ref name="Balance Point" /> and eventually replaced the astromech with [[R2-B3 (Jaina Solo)|R2-B3]], a droid nicknamed "Cappie" after her fallen wingman [[Anni Capstan]].<ref name="Rebel Dream">''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Sparky first appeared in the [[1999]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'', the second book in the series ''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order]]'' and the first installment of the [[Dark Tide duology|''Dark Tide Duology'']].<ref name="Onslaught" />

Although described as an R5 unit in ''Onslaught'',<ref name="Onslaught" /> ''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'',<ref name="Balance Point" /> and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]'', Sparky is mistakenly described as an [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]].<ref name="Ruin">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]''</ref>
